title: 9 Best Herbal Creams to Combat Frequent Headaches

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosalinic acid is good for headaches because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels, which can ease the pain and discomfort associated with headaches.
Menthol is good for headaches because it helps to reduce pain and inflammation by cooling the nerves and blocking the production of certain chemicals that cause pain.
Menthone is good for headaches because it has a natural pain-relieving effect that helps to ease and calm the mind and body, reducing the severity of headache symptoms.
Linalool is good for headaches because it has a natural pain-relieving effect that helps to calm the mind and body, reducing the severity of migraine and tension headaches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams, also known as peppermint creams, can be very helpful for headaches. Peppermint has a cooling effect on the skin, which can help to ease the pain and tension in the head and neck. The menthol in peppermint creams can also help to relax muscles and reduce inflammation. When applied to the skin, menthol can numb the area and provide quick relief from a headache. Additionally, the menthol can help to improve blood flow and reduce congestion, which can contribute to headaches. Some people find that applying peppermint creams to their temples or neck can help to ease the pain and discomfort associated with tension headaches.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with headaches, start by drying the leaves of the Salvia officinalis plant in a cool, dark place. Once dry, grind the leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine 2 tablespoons of the Salvia powder with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax has melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove the double boiler from the heat and let it cool slightly. Add 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil and a few drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely before use. To apply the cream, gently massage a small amount onto your temples or areas where you feel pain, and enjoy the soothing relief from your headaches.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for headaches

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid helps to ease headaches by reducing stress and anxiety, as well as acting as a natural pain-reliever by blocking the pain pathways in the brain.
Valeranone is good for headaches because it helps reduce pain and inflammation by blocking the action of chemicals in the brain that cause pain, which can help relieve headache symptoms.
Valerenal is good for headaches because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce pain and tension caused by headaches.
Isovaleric acid is good for headaches because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the brain and neck, which can cause and worsen headaches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams can help with headaches because it contains a natural ingredient called valerenic acid. This acid has a calming effect on the body, which can reduce muscle tension and ease pain. When we have a headache, it's often due to tension in the neck and scalp muscles. Valerenic acid in Valeriana officinalis creams works by relaxing these muscles, which can help reduce headache symptoms. Additionally, Valeriana officinalis creams may also help regulate sleep patterns, which can be disrupted by headaches. By promoting better sleep and reducing muscle tension, Valeriana officinalis creams may help alleviate headache symptoms and provide relief.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams for headaches, start by harvesting fresh or dried Salvia leaves, and dry them thoroughly if using fresh. You'll need about 1 tablespoon of dried leaves or 2 tablespoons of fresh leaves for every 8 ounces of cream. Combine the dried or fresh leaves with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, then let it steep for another 10-15 minutes. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the leaves. Combine the liquid with 8 ounces of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and let it infuse for a few hours or overnight in the refrigerator. Next, mix 8 ounces of beeswax and 8 ounces of a moisturizing ingredient like shea butter or cocoa butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the wax and butter have melted, remove from heat and slowly add the infused oil mixture, stirring constantly. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before applying it to your skin as needed.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for headaches

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Romasminic acid is good for headaches because it has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to relax blood vessels and reduce pain in the head.

Apigenin in <built-in method capitalize of str object at 0x76c3cd2a05f0> Salvia officinalis is good for headaches because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and swelling in the brain and blood vessels, which can cause headaches.
Luteolin is good for headaches because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in blood vessels, thereby relieving migraine and tension headaches.
Quercetin is good for headaches because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the blood vessels of the brain, leading to relief from headache symptoms.
Tannins are good for headaches because they help to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation in the brain, which can help to ease pain and relieve headache symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis cream, also known as lemon balm cream, is a natural remedy that can help relieve headaches. The active compounds in this cream, such as rosmarinic acid and linalool, have anti-inflammatory and calming properties. When applied topically to the skin, these compounds can help relax tense muscles and reduce pain. The cooling sensation of the cream can also help ease the pressure and discomfort associated with headaches. Additionally, Melissa officinalis cream has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help calm anxiety and stress that may trigger headaches. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, this cream can provide quick and effective relief from headache symptoms, making it a popular natural remedy for those seeking a gentle and non-invasive solution.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with headaches, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative like vitamin E oil. First, steep the dried Salvia officinalis leaves in the carrier oil in a saucepan over low heat for 2-3 hours to create a herbal infusion. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material, then measure out the desired amount of infused oil. In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat, then slowly pour in the infused oil and mix well. Add the preservative, like vitamin E oil, and stir until fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before using. Once solid, the Salvia officinalis herbal cream can be applied topically to the affected area, such as the temples or neck, to help soothe and relieve headaches.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for headaches

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for headaches because it helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the brain by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic, thereby providing relief from headaches and migraines.
Linalyl acetate is good for headaches because it has a calming effect on the body, which helps to reduce tension and relieve pain in the head and neck.

Rosmarinic acid is good for headaches because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce pain and swelling in the brain, relax tense blood vessels, and block the production of chemicals that trigger headaches.
Caryophyllene oxide is good for headaches because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the blood vessels, which are often the cause of migraine headaches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, is a popular ingredient in creams for relieving headaches. This is because it contains a chemical called linalool, which has a calming effect on the body. When you apply a lavender cream to your skin, the linalool is absorbed into your bloodstream and travels to your brain, where it can help reduce stress and anxiety. This can be especially helpful if your headaches are caused by tension or stress. Lavender cream can also help relax your muscles, which can help ease headache pain. Additionally, lavender has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the head and neck. By using a lavender cream, you can help calm your body and mind, which may help ease headache symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with headaches, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, carrier oils such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, essential oils like peppermint or lavender, and a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. First, combine the dried Salvia leaves with a carrier oil in a small saucepan and gently heat it over low heat for about 30 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse their properties into the oil. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a separate saucepan over low heat, then slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax, stirring constantly to combine. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Finally, add a few drops of your chosen essential oil and mix well. Pour the cream into small containers and let it cool completely before using it to massage onto your skin or apply it to your temples to help ease headache pain.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for headaches

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Apigenin is good for headaches because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the blood vessels of the brain, which can be a cause of headaches.
Quercetin helps with headaches because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease pain in blood vessels, which can contribute to headaches.
Luteolin is good for headaches because it helps reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels, which can ease pain and tension in the head and neck.
Tannins are good for headaches because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to relax blood vessels in the brain, reducing pain and pressure that can cause headaches.
Rosmarinic acid is good for headaches because it helps relax blood vessels, reduce inflammation, and ease pain by blocking the production of certain chemicals in the brain that cause headaches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium cream, also known as yarrow or milfoil, is a natural remedy for headaches. The active compounds in yarrow, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce pain and swelling in the head. When applied topically, the cream can ease tension and relax the muscles in the neck and scalp, which are common areas that contribute to headaches. Yarrow also has a cooling effect, which can help to reduce heat and pressure that may be causing the headache. Additionally, yarrow has antispasmodic properties, which can help to calm and relax the blood vessels in the head, reducing the frequency and severity of headaches. The cream can be applied directly to the affected area, providing quick and effective relief from headache symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal cream that can help with headaches, you will need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a cream base like shea or cocoa butter. First, steep the dried leaves in the carrier oil in a saucepan over low heat for a few hours to create a herbal infusion. Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl to separate the infused oil from the leaves. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short bursts. Combine the infused oil and melted beeswax in a bowl, then slowly add the cream base, stirring constantly until it thickens and emulsifies. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool before using it as a topical cream to help alleviate headaches by reducing inflammation and relaxing tense muscles.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for headaches

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for headaches because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce pain and inflammation in the blood vessels and brain, making them a natural remedy for relieving headache symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to help ease headaches. The active compound in fennel, called anethole, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the head and neck. When applied topically in a cream form, fennel can help relax tense muscles and calm the nervous system, which can help alleviate headache symptoms. Fennel cream can also help to ease sinus pressure and congestion, which can contribute to headaches. Additionally, fennel has a natural antispasmodic effect, which means it can help to calm muscle spasms and cramps that can cause headaches. By reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and easing sinus pressure, fennel cream can provide relief from headache symptoms and help to promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with headaches, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you'll need to dry the leaves of the Salvia officinalis plant, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator. Once the leaves are dry, you can chop them into small pieces and use a coffee grinder or spice grinder to make a fine powder. Next, combine the powdered Salvia leaves with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a heat-proof bowl. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan with simmering water, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the herbal properties of the Salvia. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any solids, then let it cool. Once the mixture has cooled, you can mix it with a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, to create a smooth and creamy texture. Finally, transfer the cream to a jar or container and store it in a cool, dark place. When you need relief from a headache, simply apply a small amount of the Salvia cream to the affected area and massage it in gently.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for headaches

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for headaches because it helps to ease pain by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the brain, which can help to relieve pressure and tension that often causes headaches.
Tannins are good for headaches because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and pain in blood vessels, which can cause headaches.
Quercetin is good for headaches because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in blood vessels, which can cause headache symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus cream is a natural remedy for headaches. It contains a substance called eucalyptol, which helps to relax muscles and ease pain. When applied to the skin, eucalyptol is absorbed into the bloodstream and reaches the brain. There, it helps to reduce inflammation and block pain signals. This can provide quick relief from tension headaches, migraines, and other types of headaches. Eucalyptol also has a decongestant effect, which can help to clear congestion in the sinuses and reduce pressure on the head. As a result, many people find that using Eucalyptus globulus cream helps to relieve their headaches and improve their overall well-being.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with headaches, start by gathering dried Salvia officinalis leaves and a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Fill a clean glass jar with the dried leaves and pour enough oil over them to cover the leaves completely. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for about two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process is called infusion and it helps the plant's medicinal properties to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and add a thickening agent such as beeswax or shea butter to the oil to thicken it into a creamy texture. Heat the mixture gently until the beeswax or shea butter has melted and the cream has reached the desired consistency. Let it cool and then transfer it to a clean glass jar. Apply the cream to the affected area as needed to help alleviate headaches.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for headaches

Tannins are good for headaches because they have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in blood vessels and ease pain, thereby providing relief from headaches.

Polyphenols are good for headaches because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the blood vessels and nerves that are commonly affected by headaches.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium cream is a natural remedy that can help relieve headaches. The active compounds in the plant, such as thujone and borneol, have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. When applied topically, these compounds can help reduce swelling and ease tension in the muscles, which can contribute to headache pain. The cream can also help relax the mind and body, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger headaches. Additionally, the cooling sensation of the cream can help numb the pain and provide quick relief. Some people find that using Artemisia absinthium cream as a topical treatment can be more effective than taking medication, as it can target the pain locally without causing systemic side effects. By reducing inflammation, easing tension, and providing a cooling sensation, Artemisia absinthium cream can be a helpful tool in managing headache pain.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with headaches, start by drying fresh Salvia officinalis leaves in a warm, dark place. Once dry, grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine 2 tablespoons of the dried Salvia powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil or other carrier oil in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil is warm and the powder has dissolved. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Once cooled, add 1 teaspoon of beeswax to the mixture and stir until it is fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into a small container, such as a tin or jar, and let it cool and harden completely. Once hardened, the cream can be applied directly to the skin, such as on the temples or neck, to help relieve headache pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis cream is made from a natural herb called sage. It has been used for centuries to help with headaches. The active ingredients in the cream, such as salvenic acid, work by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles in the neck and scalp. When these areas are tense, it can cause headaches. By applying the cream to the affected area, it helps to calm down the muscles and reduce pain. The cream also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease the pressure on the nerves. This can lead to a decrease in headache frequency and intensity. The cream is also easily absorbed into the skin, making it a convenient and non-invasive treatment option.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with headaches, you'll need to gather some fresh or dried Salvia leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, and a cream base like beeswax and shea butter. First, dry the Salvia leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area or use dried leaves if available. Next, infuse the dried leaves in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and storing it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the leaves. Then, melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each heating. Once melted, combine the infused oil with the melted cream base and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Salvia officinalis herbal cream is ready to use, simply apply a small amount to the affected area and massage it in gently. This cream can help soothe and relieve headache pain due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for headaches

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great help for people who suffer from headaches. One of the key herbs used in these creams is peppermint (Mentha x piperita), which has a cooling effect on the skin. When applied topically, peppermint oil can help relax tense muscles in the neck and scalp, which are common areas where tension can lead to headaches. This can help reduce pain and discomfort.  Another herb used in herbal creams is valerian (Valeriana officinalis), which is known for its calming properties. Valerian root can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress, both of which are common triggers for headaches. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, valerian can help prevent headaches from occurring in the first place.  Fixing the issue of headaches is crucial because they can be debilitating and interfere with daily life. When you're suffering from a headache, it can be hard to focus, work, or even take care of yourself. By using herbal creams that contain peppermint and valerian, you can take steps to prevent headaches and improve your quality of life. By reducing pain and stress, you can feel more energized and motivated, which can help you tackle daily tasks and achieve your goals.
supplementary_best: The best combination of herbal creams for remedying headaches is a blend of 'Mentha x piperita' (peppermint), 'Lavandula angustifolia' (lavender), and 'Valeriana officinalis' (valerian root). The peppermint oil in this combination helps to relax the muscles and reduce pain in the head and neck, while the lavender oil calms the nervous system and promotes relaxation. The valerian root oil works as a natural sedative, reducing anxiety and stress that can trigger headaches. This combination is effective because it addresses the physical and emotional causes of headaches simultaneously. The cooling properties of peppermint oil help to reduce inflammation and ease tension, while the calming properties of lavender oil help to quiet the mind and body. When applied topically in a cream, these oils can provide fast relief from headache symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have headaches, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One of these is cream made from Willow bark (Salix spp.) which contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. Taking high doses of salicylic acid can lead to stomach problems and interact with certain medications. Another herb to avoid is Feverfew (Tanacetum parthenium), which can cause stomach upset and allergic reactions in some people. Also, avoid creams made from Ginkgo biloba, as it can increase the risk of bleeding and interact with blood thinners. Additionally, creams containing Ginger (Zingiber officinale) should be used with caution, as ginger can irritate the skin and cause allergic reactions in some individuals. Last, avoid creams made from Cayenne pepper (Capsicum annuum), as it can cause skin irritation and increase heart rate, which can be problematic for people with certain heart conditions.
supplementary_other_remedies: Cold or warm compresses can help with headaches. Applying a cold compress to the forehead, neck, or shoulders can reduce pain by numbing the nerves. A warm compress, on the other hand, can relax tense muscles, which can contribute to headaches. This is because when muscles are tense, they can put pressure on blood vessels in the head, leading to pain.  Aromatherapy, which involves inhaling certain scents, can also be helpful. Essential oils like lavender and peppermint have a calming effect on the mind and body, which can reduce headache symptoms. However, it's essential to use these oils safely by diluting them with a carrier oil and not applying them directly to the skin.  Another natural remedy is changes in breathing patterns. Deep, slow breathing can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of headaches. This is because when we're stressed or anxious, our breathing tends to be shallow and rapid, which can trigger headaches. By slowing down our breathing, we can calm the body and mind, reducing headache symptoms.  Massage can also help with headaches. Massaging specific areas of the head and neck, such as the temples and base of the neck, can increase blood flow and reduce muscle tension. This can help alleviate headache pain by relaxing the muscles and reducing pressure on blood vessels.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience headaches often have other health issues as well. One common problem is fatigue, which can be treated with medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng. These herbs help the body cope with stress and increase energy levels.  Some people who get headaches may also have digestive problems, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). The herbs peppermint and ginger can help with IBS symptoms by soothing the stomach and reducing inflammation.  Additionally, headaches can be linked to anxiety and depression. The herbal remedy passionflower can help calm the mind and reduce anxiety, while the herb St. John's Wort can help with mild depression.  People who experience migraines often have issues with blood flow and circulation. The herb feverfew can help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, which may help prevent migraines.  Some headaches can be related to allergies and sinus problems. The herbal remedy nettle can help reduce inflammation and ease sinus congestion.  Herbal remedies can be a helpful addition to traditional medicine for people who experience headaches. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any new treatments, especially if you are already taking medication or have other health conditions.
